edges.description: CAPN15 variant effects manifest as abnormal ocular development.
CAPN15 variant effects manifest as abnormal nervous system development.
CAPN15-related disease includes cerebellar pathology and Dandy-Walker malformation in the expanded phenotype.

nodes.description: Reported biallelic missense variants are associated with developmental delay, and loss-of-function variants are associated with abnormal neurologic activity.

evidence.reference_title: Novel association of Dandy-Walker malformation with CAPN15 variants expands the phenotype of oculogastrointestinal neurodevelopmental syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Biallelic missense variants have been reported to demonstrate a phenotype of eye abnormalities and developmental delay, while biallelic loss of function variants exhibit phenotypes including microcephaly and craniofacial abnormalities, cardiac and genitourinary malformations, and abnormal neurologic activity.

nodes.description: Reported biallelic missense variants are associated with ocular abnormalities.

evidence.reference_title: Novel association of Dandy-Walker malformation with CAPN15 variants expands the phenotype of oculogastrointestinal neurodevelopmental syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Biallelic missense variants have been reported to demonstrate a phenotype of eye abnormalities and developmental delay, while biallelic loss of function variants exhibit phenotypes including microcephaly and craniofacial abnormalities, cardiac and genitourinary malformations, and abnormal neurologic activity.

nodes.description: Biallelic CAPN15 pathogenic variants are the initiating genetic lesion. CAPN15 encodes a calpain-family protease, so the proximate molecular model is abnormal CAPN15-dependent protease biology during development.

evidence.reference_title: Novel association of Dandy-Walker malformation with CAPN15 variants expands the phenotype of oculogastrointestinal neurodevelopmental syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Oculogastrointestinal neurodevelopmental syndrome has been described in seven previously published individuals who harbor biallelic pathogenic variants in the CAPN15 gene.

evidence.reference_title: Novel association of Dandy-Walker malformation with CAPN15 variants expands the phenotype of oculogastrointestinal neurodevelopmental syndrome.
evidence.snippet: We report six individuals from three unrelated families harboring biallelic deleterious variants in CAPN15 with phenotypes overlapping those previously described for this disorder.

nodes.description: A subset of affected individuals show Dandy-Walker malformation, including hypoplastic vermis, fourth ventricle enlargement, and torcular elevation, and mouse data support a role for CAPN15 in normal cerebellar development.

evidence.reference_title: Novel association of Dandy-Walker malformation with CAPN15 variants expands the phenotype of oculogastrointestinal neurodevelopmental syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Of the individuals affected, four demonstrate radiographic evidence of the classical triad of Dandy–Walker malformation including hypoplastic vermis, fourth ventricle enlargement, and torcular elevation.

evidence.reference_title: Novel association of Dandy-Walker malformation with CAPN15 variants expands the phenotype of oculogastrointestinal neurodevelopmental syndrome.
evidence.snippet: To corroborate these novel clinical findings, we present supporting data from the mouse model suggesting an important role for this protein in normal cerebellar development.

nodes.description: Eye abnormalities are a core manifestation of CAPN15-related disease.

evidence.reference_title: Novel association of Dandy-Walker malformation with CAPN15 variants expands the phenotype of oculogastrointestinal neurodevelopmental syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Biallelic missense variants have been reported to demonstrate a phenotype of eye abnormalities and developmental delay, while biallelic loss of function variants exhibit phenotypes including microcephaly and craniofacial abnormalities, cardiac and genitourinary malformations, and abnormal neurologic activity.

nodes.description: Developmental delay is a core neurodevelopmental manifestation.

evidence.reference_title: Novel association of Dandy-Walker malformation with CAPN15 variants expands the phenotype of oculogastrointestinal neurodevelopmental syndrome.
evidence.snippet: Biallelic missense variants have been reported to demonstrate a phenotype of eye abnormalities and developmental delay, while biallelic loss of function variants exhibit phenotypes including microcephaly and craniofacial abnormalities, cardiac and genitourinary malformations, and abnormal neurologic activity.

nodes.description: No disease-modifying therapy is established; care is supportive and directed to developmental, neurologic, ocular, cardiac, genitourinary, and other organ-specific findings.
